title: alnus cordifolia var. subcordata
intro: Alnus cordifolia var. subcordata, also known as subcordate mountain alder, is a plant species in the alder family (Betulaceae) that is native to the western regions of North America. The medicinal properties of subcordate mountain alder include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which make it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support wound healing, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Subcordate mountain alder can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
